zh.lifeSummary: Henry Smyth於大約1545年出生在英國英格蘭。他於大約1570年與Mrs. Brydgett Smyth在英國英格蘭諾福爾可姆斯比結婚。他們至少是2個女兒的父母。他去世於1625年8月11日之前在英國諾福爾可姆斯比，埋葬在英國英格蘭諾福爾可姆斯比。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Henry Smyth于大约1545年出生在英国英格兰。他于大约1570年与Mrs. Brydgett Smyth在英国英格兰諾福爾可姆斯比结婚。他们至少是2个女儿的父母。他去世于1625年8月11日之前在英国諾福爾可姆斯比，埋葬在英国英格兰諾福爾可姆斯比。

nameMeanings.name: Smyth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Ronan, Aidan, Brigid, Conor, Eoin, Malachy, Paddy.
English: variant of   Smith  . This form of the surname is particularly common Ireland.

variants.name: Smith
meanings: English and Scottish: occupational name denoting a worker in metal, especially iron, such as a blacksmith or farrier, from Middle English smith ‘smith’ (Old English smith, probably a derivative of smītan ‘to strike, hammer’). Early examples are also found in the Latin form Faber . Metal-working was one of the earliest occupations for which specialist skills were required, and its importance ensured that this term and its equivalents in other languages were the most widespread of all occupational surnames in Europe. Medieval smiths were important not only in making horseshoes, plowshares, and other domestic articles, but above all for their skill in forging swords, other weapons, and armor. This is also the most frequent of all surnames in the US. It is very common among African Americans and Native Americans (see also 5 below). This surname (in any of the two possible English senses; see also below) is also found in Haiti. See also Smither .

English: from Middle English smithe ‘smithy, forge’ (Old English smiththe). The surname may be topographic, for someone who lived in or by a blacksmith's shop, occupational, for someone who worked in one, or habitational, from a place so named, such as Smitha in King's Nympton (Devon). Compare Smithey .
Irish and Scottish: sometimes adopted for Gaelic Mac Gobhann, Irish Mac Gabhann ‘son of the smith’. See McGowan .

nameMeanings.name: Henry
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: A perennially popular given name, of Continental Germanic origin, from haim ‘home’ + rīc ‘power, ruler’. It was an Old French name, adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to Britain. It has been borne by eight kings of England. Not until the 17th century did the form Henry (as opposed to Harry ) become the standard vernacular form, mainly under the influence of the Latin form Henricus and French Henri.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

en.lifeSummary: Henry Smyth was born about 1545, in England, United Kingdom. He married Mrs. Brydgett Smyth about 1570, in Hemsby, Norfolk, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 2 daughters. He died before 11 August 1625, in Hemsby, Norfolk, England, and was buried in Hemsby, Norfolk, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Henry Smyth est né environ 1545, à Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Il a épousé Mrs. Brydgett Smyth environ 1570, à Hemsby, Norfolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 2 filles. Il est décédé avant 11 août 1625, à Hemsby, Norfolk, Angleterre, et a été enterré à Hemsby, Norfolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Henry Smyth wurde um 1545 in England, United Kingdom geboren. Er hat um 1570 in Hemsby, Norfolk, England, United Kingdom Mrs. Brydgett Smyth geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Töchter. Er starb vor 11. August 1625 in Hemsby, Norfolk, England und wurde in Hemsby, Norfolk, England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Henry Smyth è nato il circa 1545 nel Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Lui sposato Mrs. Brydgett Smyth circa 1570, nel Hemsby, Norfolk, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figlie. Morì prima del 11 agosto 1625, nel Hemsby, Norfolk, England, e fu sepolto a Hemsby, Norfolk, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Henry Smythは約1545年にイギリスイギリスで生まれた。約1570年にイギリスイギリスノーフォルクヘムスビーでMrs. Brydgett Smythと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の娘の親であった。彼は1625年8月11日以前にイギリスノーフォルクヘムスビーで死去し，イギリスイギリスノーフォルクヘムスビーにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Henry Smyth(은)는 대략 1545년 영국 잉글랜드에서 태어났다. 그는 대략 1570년 영국 잉글랜드 노르폴크 헤스비에서 Mrs. Brydgett Smyth(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1625년 8월 11일 이전 영국 노르폴크 헤스비에서 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 노르폴크 헤스비에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Henry Smyth nasceu aproximadamente 1545, em Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Ele casou-se com Mrs. Brydgett Smyth aproximadamente 1570, em Hemsby, Norfolk, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhas. Ele faleceu antes 11 de agosto de 1625, em Hemsby, Norfolk, Inglaterra, e foi sepultado em Hemsby, Norfolk, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Henry Smyth родился около 1545 г. в местности Англия, Великобритания. Он женился на женщине по имени Mrs. Brydgett Smyth около 1570 г. в местности хемсби, норфолк, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 2 дочери. Он умер перед 11 августа 1625 г. в местности хемсби, норфолк, Англия и был похоронен в местности хемсби, норфолк, Англия, Великобритания.
